skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: Use this skill when the user wants to produce a short video (5–120 seconds). Supports any video type: product ads, TikTok/Instagram/YouTube content, brand videos, explainers, social clips. USE FOR: video production, AI video, make a video, product video, brand video, promotional clip, explainer video, short video.
skill.summary: AI-powered video creation agent for producing short-form content (5-120 seconds)
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 534 patterns across shell scripts and documentation. After manual review, all findings are FALSE POSITIVES. Shell command patterns are legitimate API operations to pexo.ai service. Environment variables access standard config files for API keys. Documentation files contain instructional examples. No malicious intent or security vulnerabilities confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (MD5)
medium_findings.description: MD5 hash detected in scripts. Used for checksums, not security. Low risk as not used for authentication or encryption.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: MD5 pattern found but context shows it's used for non-security purposes (checksums). Not a vulnerability but should be documented.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Substitution Patterns
low_findings.description: Extensive use of $() and backticks for command substitution. All reviewed instances are legitimate API calls, file operations, or data processing. No user input injection vectors found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern is common in bash scripts. All reviewed cases use hardcoded commands or validated variables, not user input.

low_findings.title: Configuration File Access
low_findings.description: Scripts access ~/.pexo/config for API credentials. This is standard practice for user-level configuration. No privilege escalation risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard Unix convention for user config files. Appropriate permissions and location.

low_findings.title: Documentation Code Examples
low_findings.description: SETUP-CHECKLIST.md and TROUBLESHOOTING.md contain shell command examples flagged as code execution. These are instructional text, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Markdown documentation files with code blocks. These are clearly marked as examples, not executable scripts.

content.value_statement: Produce professional short videos (5-120 seconds) for social media, ads, and brand content. The AI handles scriptwriting, visuals, and editing automatically.
actual_capabilities: Generate 5-120 second videos in 16:9, 9:16, or 1:1 aspect ratios
Upload and reference images, videos, and audio files as source material
Create product advertisements, social media clips, brand videos, and explainers
Handle revision requests by referencing existing project IDs
Retrieve final video files and direct download URLs
Poll for project status and handle preview selection workflow
limitations: Cannot crawl or download files from web URLs - files must be uploaded locally
Video production takes 15-20 minutes for short content, longer for complex videos
Requires valid Pexo API credentials and available account credits
Aspect ratio must be selected from 16:9, 9:16, or 1:1 options
Does not support real-time video editing or preview during production

use_cases.title: E-commerce Product Videos
use_cases.description: Upload product images and generate promotional videos for ads or social media content
use_cases.target_user: Digital marketers and small business owners

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Creation
use_cases.description: Create engaging vertical or square videos for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: Brand Storytelling
use_cases.description: Produce explainer videos or brand introductions with AI-generated scripts and visuals

prompt_templates.title: Create Product Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30-second product video for my shoes. I have uploaded product photos.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to promote a product with existing assets

prompt_templates.title: Social Media Clip
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a vertical TikTok video about sustainable fashion tips, 15 seconds long
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs content for a specific social platform

prompt_templates.title: Brand Explainer
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an explainer video for my SaaS product. Highlight the main features and benefits
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to introduce their product to new audiences

prompt_templates.title: Revision Request
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to revise the video. Make it more energetic and add upbeat background music
prompt_templates.scenario: User has an existing project and wants changes

output_examples.input: Create a 15-second promotional video for my coffee shop
output_examples.output:
Project created: proj_abc123
Your video is being produced. Estimated time: 15-20 minutes.
Project link: https://pexo.ai/project/proj_abc123

[After polling]
Video ready! Download: https://pexo-assets.oss-us-east-1.aliyuncs.com/video.mp4?OSSAccessKeyId=xxx&Expires=xxx&Signature=xxx

output_examples.input: Upload product-photo.jpg and create a video advertisement
output_examples.output:
Asset uploaded: a_xyz789
Submitted to Pexo for production...

[After 18 minutes]
Your video is ready!
Video file: [Displays downloaded video]
Download URL: https://pexo-assets.oss-us-east-1.aliyuncs.com/ad.mp4?Expires=1234567890&Signature=xxx
Project: https://pexo.ai/project/proj_abc123

best_practices: Upload all source files first, then include asset IDs in your message using the correct tag format: <original-image>asset_id</original-image>
Wait at least 60 seconds between status polls to avoid overwhelming the API
Copy the user's request exactly to Pexo without adding creative interpretations - the AI specializes in video production
Send the full download URL with all query parameters (OSSAccessKeyId, Expires, Signature) to avoid 403 errors
anti_patterns: Do not add duration, aspect ratio, or style details that the user did not specify - let Pexo's video agent determine optimal parameters
Do not call pexo-chat.sh while the project status is WAIT - this triggers duplicate video production
Do not truncate or wrap video URLs in markdown links - send them as plain text with all query parameters intact
Do not attempt to download files from web URLs automatically - inform the user they must provide local file paths

faq.question: How long does video production take?
faq.answer: Short videos (15 seconds) typically take 15-20 minutes. Longer or more complex videos may take additional time.

faq.question: What file types can I upload?
faq.answer: Images (jpg, png, webp, bmp, tiff, heic), Videos (mp4, mov, avi), and Audio (mp3, wav, aac, m4a, ogg, flac).

faq.question: Can I revise an existing video?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide your project ID and describe the changes. Pexo will regenerate the video based on your feedback.

faq.question: Why did I get a credit error?
faq.answer: Your account has insufficient credits. Visit https://pexo.ai/home to purchase more credits, then retry your request.

faq.question: What video lengths are supported?
faq.answer: Videos can be 5 to 120 seconds long. Choose the appropriate length for your use case.

faq.question: How do I reference uploaded files in my video?
faq.answer: Upload the file first to get an asset ID, then include it in your message using tags like <original-image>asset_id</original-image>.
